captions: "Gondola" on Coniston Water, The Lake District.
View from our campsite at Coniston.
pages.text: View from our campsite at Coniston.

Ruskin wrote "There is no wealth but life."
	He also said "... happiness is increased not by the enlargement of the possessions, but of the heart; and days lengthened, not by the crowding of emotions, but the economy of them... if thus we taught, we had the ordering of our house and estate in our own hands, I believe no manner of temperance in pleasure would be better rewarded than that of making our gardens gay only with common flowers."
	He paid workmen to hew a nice house out of the solid rock hillside in his garden in order to give cold comforts to people who were ill in the local area.

pages.sketch_desc: A hand-drawn horizon/skyline sketch spanning the page, showing two contrasting profiles labelled left and right. Left section captioned 'skyline on west side of L. Coniston, slate, bare volcanic'; right section captioned 'skyline on east side of L. Coniston, limestone, trees'. The sketch illustrates the geological difference between the two sides of Coniston Water.
pages.text:
skyline on west side of L. Coniston, slate, bare volcanic

skyline on east side of L. Coniston, limestone, trees

Dry stone wall at Coniston. Alternating layers of flat slates & round limestone.

Sheep. oats original crops.

Bee skeps made out of rye + split bramble.

        The Gondola brought us back. I have never been in such a comfortable, fast, quiet small boat. The first passenger trip isn't until 11, but the men arrive at 9.0. to start the fire.

Tuesday 2nd May.

      We left at 9.10.  We drove over Wrynose Pass, steep, huge drops, into Wrynose Bottom + [strikethrough] down to Scidden Valley.

[sketch of valley cross-section] glacial valleys + the rivers have hardly worn into the granite.  In some places hanging valleys [sketch] with waterfalls.

      We walked over Hard Knott Pass + saw Hardknott Roman Fort, __Mediobogdum.__  It's on the edge of a cliff with a parade ground 3 acres, cleared + levelled, nearby.  We carried on around Harter Fell.  We had lunch at the top of steep cliffs next to the fort, looking out at Scarfell Pike.  It was a long, hot walk.  Downhill we came through forrests where men were sawing, through an old oak wood + over a beautiful packhorse bridge with deep cool rock pools on either side.  Birk Bridge carpark.

      We drove on north west + camped at __Boot__ in __Eskdale__.  The local pub provided beer, but the young, good-looking barman, whose father was a farmer up the valley, took ages to

[marginal note rotated, running vertically along right edge:] Wast Water, deepest lake in England, facing east

pages.sketch_desc: A hand-drawn sketch map of Lake District fells fills most of the right page. The map shows a ridge line running roughly north-south. Labels identify (from top to bottom on the left ridge): Haystacks, Great Gable, Kirk Fell. To the right is a separate ridge/valley labelled Whatdale Head (could be Wasdale Head). Scafell is labelled faintly at the top right. The diagonal caption 'Wast Water, deepest lake in England, facing east' appears on the left margin (carried over from left page). A lower caption reads 'These 3 hills symbol of Lake District', referring to the three main fell outlines depicted.
pages.text:
calculate the cost of 2 half-pints of beer.
Wed      The campsite smells beautiful of damp vegetation + cows.  __Hollin Farm__ 09467-23253  We walked up early to see the [?16c] corn mill but it wasn't open until 11.  The terminus of the Ravenglass/Eskdale railway is at Boot.

[hand-drawn route sketch/map of Lake District fells, with labels:]
Scafell [label top right, faint]
Haystacks [label top left of map]
Great Gable [label middle left]
Whatdale Head [label middle]
KirkFell [label lower left]

[caption rotated diagonally, lower left:] Wast Water, deepest lake in England, facing east
[caption lower:] These 3 hills symbol of Lake District

WHITE MOSS HOUSE

Rydal Water
Tel. Grasmere 35295

WEDNESDAY 3 MAY 1995

Carrot, coriander, lentil and orange soup
****
A bombe of brill with a centre of Bonchester Brie wrapped in smoked salmon
Sorrel and watercress sauce
****
Rack of Westmorland Herdwick spring lamb roasted with a herb crust of parsley, sage, rosemary and thyme (served pink unless otherwise requested)
Recurrant, mint and merlot sauce
Potato splits
Aubergines cooked with sesame oil and three peppers
Steamed cauliflower
Stir fried fine green beans
****
Sticky chocolate hazelnut cream slice
Cabinet pudding with fluffy lemon sauce (a hot, light steamed lemon pudding coated in raisins)

Sticky chocolate hazelnut cream slice
Cabinet pudding with fluffy lemon sauce (a hot, light steamed lemon pudding coated in raisins)
Banana ice cream
****
British cheeses with White Moss oat biscuits
***
Coffee

May we suggest a glass of Brown Brothers' Orange Muscat and Flora 19[illegible] with your dessert? £3.50

To avoid discomfort to others, guests are requested not to smoke in the dining room

pages.sketch_desc: A hand-drawn map of the Lake District in the lower half of the page. Places labelled include (reading roughly left to right and top to bottom): Ravenglass (bottom left), Wast Water (left, with a lake shape), Scafell Pike (centre-left, marked with a triangle symbol), Cliffe (centre-left, also marked with a triangle), Coniston (centre, with a small lake shape), Hawkshead (centre), Ulverston (centre-right), Grasmere (top centre), Ambleside (top centre), Rydal Water (top centre-right), [?Troutbeck] (top right area), Kendal (right), Barrow (bottom centre). Coastline is sketched along the left and bottom edges. Small circles mark lakes/tarns. The map has no explicit arrows but the spatial arrangement suggests a Lake District area overview.
pages.text:
We drove up to Wast Water & walked in the village, seeing St. Olaf's tiny church where climbers are buried & drystode walls of the farm. Some lush farm lands to the south of Wast Water. ~~For supper we went~~ We drove back via the coast road. For supper we went to the White Moss Inn - a truely delicious meal. We camped at __South Wray Castle Farm__ * * * * an amazing camp site with a meandering stream, bog & field - Was the stream for the Castle children to play on?

    __Thu May 4th.__ We drove to Dove Cottage at Grasmere, coming in on a very steep road, 25% of 1 in 4 above the white Moss Inn.

The cottage + museum were very interesting with well-informed guides. The Dove Cottage had been a pub - the Dove + Olive. The Wordsworths - William, Mary + Dorothy + Mary's sister, Anne, finally moved out because there wasn't enough room. Coleridge visited + fell in love with Anne, inspite of being married himself, which Wordsworth regarded as an "inconvenience." They lived at Dove Cottage from 1799-1808 + Wordsworth wrote most of his best poetry while living there. We looked around the church. + at the Wordsworth graves + the tourist shops + then drove off into the countryside to have lunch in the van near Troutbeck.
    We visited Townend, __Troutbeck__, for 400 years home of the Browne family. George Brown d.1914, + his 3rd daughter died 1943. It has been a NT house since 1947. There is amazing carved oak furniture in all the rooms - clocks, beds, wainscoats, bookcases. Troutbeck is a very pretty village - groups of houses. At 4.30 we set off for home.

We had supper at Jonathan's on the way - broccoli, cheddar, Stilton, Butterscotch icecream + vanilla sauce.
    Wordsworth was left £900 by a friend whom he nursed while he died of consumption. Wordsworth invested the money, to receive £80 p.a., of which £8 pa was paid in rent for Dove Cottage. The Grasmere vicar earned £40 pa at this time.

Saturday May 27th
    We left, the 7 of us at 8.20, at 9.0 we stopped for breakfast on Bewdley by-pass. It's advertised as a car park for the Worcs. Way. We drove on along roads, not motorways, through very heavy traffic + only arrived at __Upper Cantref Farm__ at 12.00. The boys played cricket. Peter + [?Thomas] rode in the afternoon. It started raining just as William + Simon finished putting up the tents. Simon + Eve + John walked down to [the village] [?Llangynach]. It is extraordinarily windy + [Simon]

kept sitting up in bed to check the tents. John slept in the cab + Charles slept on the top bunk for the first time. John + Charles are the same age that William + Peter were when first we started camping.
__Sunday 28th May__
	At 9.30 John + Charles rode around the ring on leading reins led by the rest of us. John started learning to post to the trot.
	At 10.30. Thomas, William + Peter set off on a day trip. It didn't stop raining. At 2.40. they came back without having stopped for a picnic lunch.
	Eve, John + Charles went off to Evensong + Thomas + William had a fight in the gangway + broke William's glasses.
	There was a sexton, his wife + the vicar + we sang hymns unaccompanied.
	After tea the sun came out, we tried to dry the clothes + played cricket.
	John + Charles had baths in the shower [illegible]. Peter + Eve showered.
__Monday__ Thomas + [William crossed out]Peter [?William written above] showered in the morning. It's still raining a lot. There are ewes lambing in this field. The farmer woman comes to visit them every few hours. Before lunch one

of the ewes had one lamb, + after lunch there were two.	John + Charles went on horseback in the ring [?with a leading rein].
	Thomas + Peter set off on a day ride with Jean, the head horse woman (really a local school teacher) William, John, Charles, [?Junior] + Eve set off to Brecon. We stopped at the White Swan __Llanfrynach__, to book a meal of 7 at 7.. At Brecon we telephoned Joan Clarke, cancelling the barbeque + inviting ourselves to tea tomorrow. We bought good-quality waterproofs for T, W, P. We bought charcoal, bread + milk. We should have brought one loaf per day, + we had forgotten the tinned milk. On the way back to Cantref we stopped in the road to look at the Blackham's house.
	Having started lunch outside, we moved the table into the tent as it started to shower. In the afternoon the boys played cricket with Simon + Charles + Eve collected sheep pooh.
	At 6.15 we set off to walk the 1½ miles to the restaurant. We got there early, + followed a sort of mill-race back through a park towards [to] a Roman Bathhouse.
	Supper was delicious - onion soup, Welsh lamb chops + caramel cream. We staggered home.

Tuesday 30th May
	No rain so far this morning. John wanders up to look at the horses. He loves the dogs & cats. There is a dog, corgi, called Holly. All 7 of us are setting out on a ride at 10.30.
	The ride was marvellous. There were 11 of us altogether. We went along trails & narrow stream beds & hillsides. Twice one of the guides went ahead with John, Charles & Simon & the rest of us held back for a canter. John has already learnt to rise to the trot & Charles bounces about like a relaxed pea. There were lovely views & it didn't rain.
	Unfortunately William fell off onto his nose on the last canter. (Superman fell off the day before & broke his neck).
	We had lunch, packed up & drove off to have tea with Joan Clarke. Then we went on for fish & chips at Malvern & dropped Thomas. We got home, showered the children & Eve was in the bath when John W & Hannah & 3 boys arrived!
	Inspite of the rain, it was a super half-term camping trip. (& 200 for camping & riding)

Sunday June 18
	__Simon, Eve, Thomas, William, John + Charles went to Middleton Hall for Mary Cohen's afternoon concert.__ Peter cathedralled & lunched at Susan & Simon Smith's. William watched rugger on TV in the van. Thomas played his violin. Charles, Simon & Eve listened to all the concert. Mark Hick joined William. It was a lovely sunny afternoon. We had a picnic lunch.

Saturday 24th June
	__We left at 9.05 for Northamptonshire.__ Simon, Eve, Karoleia, William, Peter, John, Charles. We had coffee on the way at __Nasby__. Fairfax & Ireton won the battle against Prince Rupert. Dead Bodies lay all the way to Leicester. The 350th anniversary was on June 14th. It happened 1645.
	It is most disappointing that it is raining slightly & not very warm.
	We arrived at __Belton-in-Rutland__ *** at 11.45. There is a pretty farm house with horses & portabloos. About 15 caravans.

John & Charles play on the see-saw & rope ladder.
    We had lunch inside, because it is horribly damp, windy & cold, & then walked up to look at the church. The Gough family gave a window to St. George. There is a face with donkey ears, sticking its tongue out in the piscina. The font is 13 or 14.
    We continued on 1½ miles around the village & countryside. 5 horses stood around Eve in a field. Simon carried Charles over the nettles. Peter felt ill & William listened to rugger on the radio so they both stayed behind at the van. We had played cricket when we got back. Most people had left the campsite to cycle around Rutland Water.
    In the evening we had a barbeque - lamb chops, sausages, & veg & beef kebabs.
    __Sunday June 25th__
    Church at 9.0. Carefully modulated tones. Returned to wash up, elevenses, & put away tents.

captions: Sunny supper at Upper [?Cautres] after a very wet days' riding
pages.text:
John wailing over writing a butterfly poem.
[photo caption, rotated left margin:] Sunny supper at Upper [?Cautres] after a very wet days' riding
    After failing to get lunch at a village pub, we ate at a coaching inn in Uppingham & returned home in the sun.

Sunday July 2nd.
      Simon & Eve took supper over to __Malvern common__ for Jim, Margaret, & Helen & Reg Farrer. Thomas came running up late. Thomas played Mozart Rondo in C in Big School. Mary Cohen & Jim & Margaret & Helen & Mary came back for a cup of tea & cashew nuts after the concert.

11am Tuesday July 25th
      Simon, Eve, Thomas, Peter, John & Charles set off for Spain via Venetia & Southampton. (William in Colorado & Alaska). See map near index.
      We had tea at Venetia's, seeing Olivia, George, Tilla & Archie. Laura & Jack left Cordelia & Helena to play. We had a lovely tea in the hot sun. But V has just been burgled, the guinea pig has died, the puppy stolen & they've building a horrible commerical shed in the next-door field. The new cottage next door is being done up, with the fellowes painting it.
      The ferry left at 8 pm & got in at 7am on Thu. It was rather sickening.
      We motored to __Castro Urdiales__. We walked up to the church which had

an interesting frieze of sculptures around the top. The town had lovely balconies & a market where we bought food. John & Charles went in the playground. Thomas bought Cadbury's sweets when we had coffee.
      We had lunch & swam at Mogro. The tide was coming in & covering up John's & Charles' holes in the sand. Peter had a lilo. The current was strong.
      We drove on to a campsite __Santillana de Mar__. The view is of a grassy hillside. There is a pool & restaurant & playground & lots of Brits in Key Camps. In the evening Simon & Eve walked past a field with cows in down to the "prettiest village in Spain." The courtiers from Cadiz built stone houses there, now lived in by farmers. It is beautiful in all directions. (St. Juliane = Santillana)

Friday 28th July
      We rigged up shade & Eve washed clothes in the machine.

      We swam, had lunch, napped, swam, had icecream, a cup of tea, and at 5.30. pm. set off down the hill for

captions: St. Juliana taking the demon captive.
pages.text:
the village. It is glorious. We went to the diocesan museum, which has one of the most beautiful cloisters I have ever seen. The collegiate church has wonderful Romanesque late [12] carvings.

[photo of stone relief carving]

St. Juliana taking the demon captive.

A good hotel to stay in would be Paradores, Playa Ramon Pelayo 8, 39330 Santillana del Mar, Cantabria. 942-81-80-00. First or 2nd floor
Fax 942, 81-83-91.

There are 4 apostles + a Panto Creator too.

John bought a penknife + we nearly bought a goatskin. We had a delicious supper with white bean soup with a few blobs of sausage, + a pudding of semolina + honey cake.

There are also the Altimira Caves near by, but we have not gone to them.

__Saturday 29th July__

After early sun, a mist surrounded the campsite. We left early in the damp + drove westward, stopping to shop at San Vincente de Barche, where Peter bought a pen knife + we tasted cheeses + sausage at the market. The town was built on a good defensive position on 2 estuaries. We had lunch inland, + found a good swimming river for the boys to try out 2 rubber boats which we [?just] bought.

Peter + John fought Thomas in the boats. Charles fell out of the boat, reaching for the paddle + swam to the edge.
	We ended up in a squalid campsite for the night __Cova Donga__ *. Dirty. Loo paper around. People leaving early in the night for work. We had a super super at Restaurant __Fabada__ where we had a friendly waiter. Peter + Thomas ate enormous amounts! Nice, clean.
	__Sunday__
	We went up to Cova Donga. It was ghastly. People had given lovely jewelry, named + dated + the Church had locked it up in glass cases. A chieftain, Pelayo, had beaten the Moors for the first time in 722. A Visigoth descendant, he united Spain to drive the Moors out. He is celebrated as a sort of King Arthur/Alfred. The monuments + chapels were built 1880. + stirred up nationalism. The church was firmly shut for the service, + Simon was wearing shorts, so we didn't attempt to go. It's enough to make you turn Quaker. Spaniards rather cold.
	We went to the zoo afterwards, which was good in parts, but had 4 unhappy pacing brown grizzly bears. Apparently they are wild on the mountains.
	We ran out of wine, + opened a

captions: Peter + John,        Thomas + Charles in boats.
pages.text: Peter + John,		Thomas + Charles in boats.

pages.sketch_desc: Hand-drawn mountain profile/skyline sketch at the top of the page, showing the view looking East across the Picos after crossing the pass at Ponton. Shows a rough outline of mountain ridges with vertical lines suggesting cliffs or slopes on the left.
captions: looking East across the Picos after crossing the pass at Ponton
pages.text:
looking East across the Picos after
crossing the pass at Ponton

bottle with a bear + honey on for lunch.
It turned out to be brandy. By the time Eve
had drunk Charles's + Thomas's + her own, she
was in a much better mood. We had lunch
by grassy meadowland + apple trees near a
salmon river.
	We drove on up River Sella, a gorgeous
gorge, with the peaks partly in cloud. We
crossed over at Ponton Pass, 1280 m. low on
petrol.
	In the north it was green + they grew
maize. There were cows near the pass. Once
over the pass the scenery is yellower with
Med Scrub.
	We stopped to have tea at Riano which is on
a mega lake, a small marina + modern houses

red tiled roof
hooks for onions
door to store maize
hanging [?EP]
wood beams
stilts with mushroom
baffles
steps

Anti-rat house. horreos.
north of Picos. For storing crops

	We drive on along a flat valley,
very hot with the sun in our eyes. Most
villages have a stork's nest on the church. * *
On to Mansillas Los Mulas + camp at __Esla__
small, pretty clean. They are building tennis courts.
	Monday 31st July	[?Irrigation from the lakes/dams make]
				[?the first part of the plain fertile.]
	We bought coffee at the Bar, on the
campsite, because we've run out of
coffee. We reached Leon, which we liked.
Until the beginning of this century, the pop.
was 30,000. Now it is [strikethrough: 100,] 130,000, so there is
a band of modern flats around the old town.
The cathedral dominates cf Beauvais, but many
flying buttresses, so it didn't fall down.
Grills around monuments - Charles's question to
me "Mummy, why don't they let you see
Jesus?"
3 storks' nests on cathedral.

3 times on the predella space under a [promi-] bishop I saw a woman distributing bread to 3 beggars/cripples.

[hand-drawn stick figure sketches of figures]

but goodness knows what the story was.

  Eve went to the museum for an hour + saw carvings, particularly mother-+-child with similar family features, from the surrounding parish churches __XII__ carved from logs of wood. I hope the churches have good copies. Simon looked after the boys.

  At 1.15. we had lunch in a café bar – very friendly, Paella, potato omlette, cream caramel + rough wine which the Romans must have [lo-] drunk. Charles got the mopes. Becau[se] he didn't get the opportunity to choose his own food.

We stopped in a pine wood something [illegible] of incense for tea, it was too hot, + then found a supermarket. Simon shopped with the children + packed the van for 1½ hours.

  We had great trouble finding the campsite, not at Val de Verda as marke[d] but at the village beyond. __S. Martin__ *****

  It is lovely: very pretty, with the opportunity to camp in the woods in the dark, or clustered around the shower blocks. Everyone, except Simon, swam in the river with the new dinghy before supper at 9.30.

  Tuesday Aug 1st.
  Clear skies, followed by cloud, followed by sun at 11.00, as we set off [down] to the river. Simon stopped a woman + her children from throwing stones off the artificial dam. Charles is making holes + channels in the "sandy beach". Simon + Thomas, Peter + John have disappeared off upstream in the boats.

  The 4 boys played in the river morning and afternoon. We had lunch – chicken, ham[?] [illegible]

eggs, ice creams, in the camp restaurant. In the evening we walked in the village. Old slates on the roof, balconies. The exterior of the church being pointed, steps up to the bells + a wobbly balcony. Pretty, lush fields up to the road, irrigation ditches, maize, tomatoes, peppers, melons, gladioli. Several new houses being built, as a result of the new motorway?

__Wed 2nd Aug__
	We got up late. Again the boys play with boats in the river. Simon + Thomas had a grand fight. Charles got frightened in the boat + swam to the shore. We had lunch at the camp restaurant again, + went back to the river afterwards. The camp laundry service washed the towels + clothes (but did not dry) in their machine for 675 ptas. The boys watched a bull fight on T.V. At 9.40. Thomas + Eve went into the village to sketch. Just as we had finished drawing the church, stork arrived, so we went back to draw them too.

__Thu 3rd Aug__
	We set off to [struck: Santiag] Santiago, visiting 2 churches on the way. We saw OCebreiro and Portmarin. OCebreiro is high up, with lovely views of hills, on the Pilgrimage route. The houses in the hamlet are old. Some of the thatched rooves with plaited ropes over them are this shape [sketch]. One had a museum showing a living room/hall, bedroom + kitchen. The church was
church with stork's nest  Villamartin

[photo of stone carving of angel with horn]

Santiago de Compostella

lovely with their shape in the stones supporting the roof beams. St. Rocca appears often along the trail. In this chapel there were hundreds of flowers.

Later we visited Portomarin. The valley was flooded for controlling water for irrigation, + the church was brought up, stone by stone, to a higher village. The stones still had numbers on. The archit. + carving were again beautiful Romanesque.

We reached __Compostella__ + camped as __As cancelas__ ***, at 7.0.. The rain is falling, so we have not put up the tent. We set off on the bus for the city centre. The cathedral is [?wonderful]. The first place in Spain that the church seems user-friendly. [?Mass] is being celebrated, + tourists swarm all around, admiring, gawping, eating crepes, achieving their goal. Many people praying. Many walk up behind the altar to kiss the

statue, & down into the crypt to see the silver caskett with St. lago's bones. Glorious Romanesque architecture & a golden baldachino. The sculptures are beautiful too.
      We had an enormous Paella Supper with all sorts of sea food. Then back to the van.
   We passed many walkers, pilgrims, on the way here.
   __Friday 4th Aug__
      At 10.30. We went of again on the bus & saw the cathedral. Eve saw sculpture & the cloister & museum. There are 5 alabasters from Nottingham. We had lunch in the street, looked at the cathedral again, bought mouth organs for T, P, J + C & walked to St. Maria del Mar, an amazing church with pillars leaning out wards & tremendous buttresses. There was a pretty cloister too, and a playground.

We caught a bus back to the van & had Santiago cake for supper.
[musical notation drawn here]

__Saturday 5th August__
   We packed up & left in cloud & drove w.S.w. through hills to the coast. We passed many oblong horreros. The peasants, on the whole, seem poor. They use scythes & barrows for collecting hay: oxen pull wagons, occasionally more often very old tractor. Beautiful stone buildings. We gave a lift to 4 swimmers/surfers heading for the coast. We had a delicious lunch - 2 crabs among 3 of us, followed by Beeffor 6 [?] O Altrovido at Paesta, recommended by "Backroads of Spain." We drove through many eucalyptus plantations & maize.
   We arrived at __Leis__ on the bay above the Ria Caminares. The town is across the bay. At 5 we went down through the pine woods for a swim. There are a few bungalows here, & a playground. A beautiful site for ou[r]

van with a view of the setting sun across the bay. There were 2 life guards in orange patrolling the beach on foot, + an inflatable motor boat with 4 more life guards patrolled the bay. Thomas + Peter practised Baywatch drill, falling out of the boat by the beach. Heaven help their victims. Charles help Eve collected seaweed for the allotment. John + Charles built sand castles on the beach. It is lovely, + not too crowded.
	Simon prepared food. The Spanish seem to have lunch at about 2.30. + supper at 10.

__Sunday__
	We swam + set off at 1.0. high at 2.45. We are going south along the coast. One village with ugly smokey factory chimneys. All the other villages very pretty small campings, small beaches + fishing villages. Rafts, 60 of them in one bay, probably with ropes hanging down for muscles to breed on. Muros would be the most interesting old village to stay in.
	We stopped for lunch by the sea

horreos

By tea time at 5.30., we had turned inland. Maize grown everywhere. Almost every old farm house has its horreos.
	We crossed into Portugal, after Eve drove a lightening tour in Vigo. We had to drive until 9.50. pm to find the only campsite around - in __Braga__ *
	From the sublime to the ridiculous. We are parked on a spare corner of ground next to a pile of road gravel. Cheek by jowl throughout the campsite, but at least no one quite next to us. The wash house like an abattoire. But a glorious view, first of the sunset across the city, then of skyscrapers sparking in dawn sun - churches, working cranes.
	Because it was Sunday evening we have been unable to change [illegible].

Monday 6 April August
		loud bangs & fireworks across city at midnight. Birtugese drivers have the panache of the Italians, but the roads aren't as safe.
		We packed up & set of south for Oporto.
Chaotic map reading. We parked in the higgledy-piggledy old quarter at the top of the hill & walked down to the harbour. The houses are close together, balconies, 3 tiers of lots of family washing. Houses covered in very pretty tiles.
		We visited St. Francis Church. Amazeing. Covered in a gold fungus. For the poor saint. A wooden sculpture of 5 Franciscans having their heads chopped off by Moors. In the museum nice lamps on lampposts with little cherubs.
		The old harbour is pretty. Original warehouses for port brought down in pink-type boats, later by train. Sandman black man? Children jumping off the quay.
		We had lunch in a family-run restaurant. Mama with 3 daughters in the kitchen & father with 2 sons as waiter.
		Old English port buildings solidly-built, could be in Sham.
		The temperature is perfect in the shady streets. Lots of interesting bridges; one of cars above & cars below. A railway. And the coast motorway being

built. We went inland, up the R. Douro, to Cinfaes, which I would like to see more of, then south east, up a tributary to the right to Castro Daire **** , a member of Orbiter camps.
There was a pool outside the campsite.
		We went through country side with large boulders, & vines edging fields. Maize. Amazing view of ridges of mountains to the south. Very tiring zig zag roads. Portuguese drivers take horrendous risks.
		The campsite smells of beautiful pines.

Tues 7 April August
		We set off south towards Guarda. Large lorries carrying enormous blocks of stone. Large boulders by the side of the road.
		__Castelo Mendo__, near the old road, we stopped at. [?Magnificent] old walls. Houses clustered inside. 2 vans selling visited the village. 3 people were filling up bottles at the 2 village taps. 3 churches, 2 locked & one open – roof has fallen in. The Sacristy had 2 beds built into the wall. Hermit? Pillars & balconies on some houses. Very has-been & cut off, with old peasant women in black. But 2 young families, too. 16 large stone blocks on top of each other – height of walls. Wellington lived in a house near, for 1 year.
		We arrived at __Ciudad Rodrigo__, & saw the cathedral with good Romanesque carvings, even a

captions: BODEGAS DUEÑAS — printed map showing LOS ARRIBES, VITIGUDINO, EL CAMPO CHARRO, CIUDAD RODRIGO, LA ALBERCA, SIERRA DE FRANCIA, las Hurdes
pages.text:
[Glued-in map/postcard: BODEGAS DUEÑAS — printed map showing Duero region, with labels including LOS ARRIBES, VITIGUDINO, SALAMANC[a], EL CAMPO CHARRO, CIUDAD RODRIGO, LA ALBERCA, SIERRA DE FRANCIA, las Hurdes, Portugal]

See map near inside

the foot of the columns. The square was pretty, people dancing and celebrating, we had icecream. Vanbanesque town walls. John dropped a pebble over. * * * *
   At 7, we arrived at la [?Aberca] campsite. It had a pale turquoise swimming pool + lovely views of hills. But the campsite people seem lazy. They don't pick up litter or mend taps, or locks. We swam. We picked blackberries. There were vanilla trees, peach, sweet chestnut + oak trees.
   The shop was shut, so we couldn't buy wine for supper, or bread. We had blackberries for supper. And then porridge oats + milk + sugar + more blackberries. It was delicious.
   Wed 8th August
      We decided to go on a circular tour. We

shopped at [?Tamames], where there is a storks' nest + stork on the church.
   We drove on to [?Miranda] + had a delicious lunch. John finished mine. The Gazpacho was very good. We walked around, the village, looking up side roads. Overhanging balconies where you could touch across the alleys, one supported on pillars. Little squares. We weren't sure which was the church. Escutcheons + 4 herds of goats on the walls.
   We went on to __El Gaseo__ in [?Casthuerdo], very cut-off village. Poor people, but generous, they gave us tomatoes + pears, + all we could give them was some chocolates. The houses cling to the cliff, some are round with big, irregular slates on the roof. These now house the donkeys + chickens, + the people are in modern houses. Steep paths. The river is at the bottom. All along the river, there are runnels off to irrigate the crops in the river bends.
   We came home up hairpin bends to a pass __El Portillo__, amazing views. Horrific drops. (We missed out "La Lena" the highest hill, with a monastery on the top.) We had a swim + then prawns for supper, which was a bit of a disaster because wasps swarmed in.
   Earlier in the day, Thomas, Peter, John + Charles had done hard washing of their clothes

One of the trees in the campsite seemed to be carob. It was dropping brown stick drip 9. Only the tent. [?a bit]  their suction pull over [?pest] 9. fall 9 [?from] [illegible] [?brambels]. After ages S pulled him out on a rope.
	In the night there was distant lightening over the Behar Hills, Sierra de Behar, + slight rain. So we brought in the washing. Besides this, a bird flew in through the open back doors of the van + pecked the washing above Eve's head. So it was not a restful night.
	__Thu 9th Aug__
		We stopped for gas at Tamames, + Eve saw the church, clean + simple + cheerful inside.
		On through cattle country. Holm oak trees dot the fields as far as you can see. Why? Large farms. Corrals for cows, mostly

[wine label glued in: BODEGAS DUEÑAS / VINO DE MESA / TINTO PUENTE / EMBOTELLADO POR BODEGAS DUEÑAS, S.A. / Pol. El MONTALVO, Tlf. 27638-212980 / CARBAJOSA DE LA SAGRADA-Salamanca (España) / 73 cl. / GRADO ALCOHOLICO 13% Vol. / R.E.N.º 2906·SA · R.S.N.º 30166/SA]

Old Roman bridge, Salamanca.

[sketch of oak tree: Oak tree carefully pruned - large branches spread out.]
[sketch of tall tree with label: caro tree?]

	We parked outside __Salamanca__ + walked in across the Roman Bridge. Charles watched for lizards. storks, rooks + storks
	We looked around the old cathedral. Scaley roof. Romanesque carvings + old wall-paintings; cloister. We had the best meal of the trip at __San Ricardo__, inside pink granite portals. Even good puddings. I had spinache, egg & prawn + caramel custard. And some of this bottle of wine. We saw the square. A good city.
	Eve took Peter, John + Charles back to the van, so that Simon + Thomas could [illegible] + shop. A storm was coming. Eve thought it wouldn't rain. Simon + Thomas came running + then the heavens opened. We sat in a [?cageby] [illegible] twice.
	On eastward. The countryside grows wheat + some sunflowers. Richer. Modern farm machinery [illegible] for the first time. Villages use brick

We stopped at El Astral at Tordesillas nr to Valladolid The storm came close so the boys weren't allowed to swim at first. Then we all swam, except Simon, who wasn't feeling & very well after all yesterday's fruit & gas pacho. [marginalia: bisomething of] (campersham to h. Auvero again like [?Portugal])
So [marginalia: Friday 10th Aug] [marginalia: breakfast something] we left at 10.35. Simon's better.
The loos don't seem as clean as France. Dirty water left around in mop buckets (not at El Astral).
ch Broken things. Men don't mind. Broken brackets & screws on children's playgrounds. 2 out of 3 lav. do locks broken. Shower taps fall off. Home-made or badly designed shower cubicles. Not enough hooks.
A We stopped for lunch, + quickly moved on because of 8 wasps arriving in 2 minutes. We next stopped in a field of poplars near Lerma. Fewer wasps.
A disappointing afternoon. 2 church visits, + we couldn't get in. The first, 3.15 Covar Rubias, was a church which was shut until 4.30. The village was pretty, a __monastery__ is there, but we drove on.
The second had a plain church which we saw, + a beautiful cloister, but the queue was too long. San Domingo de Silos, a

monastery is still there.
We carried on to El Frontal [?++++] at Abegar. Expensive, a lovely clover-shaped swimming pool + a [strikethrough: con] canopy of pines. Mini football. There is a lake, but we haven't been there.
Sat 11th Aug
We have spent a lazy day at the pool. Very pleasant. OK. lunch in camp restaurant, but horrible rock music.
Simon cooked delicious tom. + egg fry-up for supper, + next door campers blared out more rock music at full volume until Simon + Thomas asked them to switch it off. Nice not having any dau.
Sun 12th Aug
We left at 10.30. Bought petrol. As we got to Molinos to go to church, everyone was filing out. It was an upstairs room. Too bad, it looked merry with lots of children. On to Vinuesa, a nice church, candlelit. Balconies in the village. Boys playing tennis on half a court + wall. Wash house, long + low, with river, mill-race, flowing through. Stones for women to kneel + rub clothes. Paddock at the back. We went over a very high pass, [?5] 1 nes. On to where there was a good restaurant, lot

For May half-term need woolly hats, barbeque, meat, charcoal, a loaf a day, tinned milk, loo paper
at 2.30. it was full. So we drove on looking for a place to make out our own lunch in the shade, but the gorge was so steep we didn't find one. Finally we found a restaurant + had lunch at 3.30.
		We've stopped in our last campsite in Spain. It is very clean + has a municipal pool nearby. __Najera__		**** We swam + Thomas + Ster rowed, so we are not looking around the town. The best campsite we've been in this holiday - We are in a circular courtyard.
		After supper we went out for a walk + joined the promenaders. Festive crowds in the town, John + Charles + I joined in some dancing.
	Mon 13th
	We got up at 8 + left at 9.45. Through terraced Rioja country. Unfortunately it rained + wet the tent. We reached Bilbao in time. There were long queues on the quai.
	__Tues 14th 1995__
		We set off from Portsmouth for Birmingham at 4.40. pm.

[Glued-in black-and-white photograph of a Romanesque tympanum, likely from __Vézelay__ basilica]
[?Sennit] 523-6668
[?compositor]
__Vézelay__

captions: BORGUND STAVKYRKJE / FORENINGEN / TIL NORSKE FORTIDSMINNES- / MERKERS BEVARING / Kr. 100,- / 2880
pages.text:
Kirby  86 Westfield Rd, Edgbaston Birmingham B1533[?b]
August 31st 1995

Log 3

[glued-in ticket: BORGUND STAVKYRKJE / FORENINGEN / TIL NORSKE FORTIDSMINNES- / MERKERS BEVARING / Kr. 100,- / 2880]
£1 = NK12

1. Bromyard. Farnham 1995
   Hartley Wintney
2. London
3. Beauchamps Gloucester 1996
   Calver Mill, Peaks
5. Chatsworth
7. Farnborough Hall
8. Stratford
9. Mersea, Colchester, Doncaster,
   Thorpeness
10. Anglesey, Dun Laoghaire
11. Glendalough. Kildare, Kilkenny.
12. Rock of Cashel, Micheltown.
13. Dingle, [?Micheltown], Gallarus
14. Dunquin
15. Great Blasket, Camp
16. Lahinch, Moher, Millvee, Burren
17. Map of Ireland, Leamaneh.
18. Ennis, Clonmacnoise, Lough Ree
19. Grandard
20. Killyleagh, Seaforde, Portaferry.
   Karcutt [?Kircutt].
21. Beauchamp, Stanford Bridge
22. London, Ypres
23. Kortrijk. Oudenaarde
24. Gent

26. Beauchamp

39. [?Cassahiouse] [?Celurnu]
40. Conques
41. [?Vieuville]
42. Entragues [?Entraygues]
43. Avon [?Armand]
   La Malène
45. St. Enimie
46. Monastier / St. Jean [illegible]
47. La Belle [?Eche]  [?Vaux]
48. Channel Tunnel
49. Beauchamp
50. Malmesbury
   East of Welland
51. London Greenwich
   Imp War Museum
   __Tate__ 1998
55. Oundle
56. Beauchamp, [?Worcstr]
58. Abbey Dore, Golden Vall[ey]
59. Hanley Swan.
61. [?Bnas] [?Fetincy]
62. Machynlleth
   Newcastle, Bergen
64. Dale
65. Laerdal, [?Borgand]
66. [?Sognafjord], [?Schwengabb]
68. [?Nigardsbreen] glacier
   [?Arde], [?Urnes]
69. Bergsdalen, [?Ringebu]
   [?Trajvang]
70. Koppang, [?Holjes]
71. [?Humletorp] [?Marbeck]
72. Sunne, Oslo
74. [?Konasberg]
75. [?Rjukan] [?Vemost]

76 Setesdal
77 Sirdal
   Stavanger
78 North Shields
   Lincolnshire
   Conway
79 Dolwyddelan
   Llangollen
   Llandudno
80 Beauchamp

August 31st 1995.

   We were about to leave at 6.0. but Eve alarmed John in the house. It was 8 before the alarm men managed to reset it. Simon & Eve, Thomas 15, William 13, John 8, Charles 6, left for Bromyard, Kate & Jamie's farm. (Peter at Beauchamp).
   The Sands, Peter + his were at the Farm with their children, before leaving for Australia.

__Friday__
   We watched the grain lorry loading with grain. The boys saw Edward's 2 Vietnamese pigs being fed. We swam & played tennis & picked Blackberries. A lovely day.
   Kate & Jamie & Ben & Edward joined us for supper.

__Saturday__
   Edward & Jamie beat Thomas & Eve at tennis, just. (Simon & Eve had beaten Thomas & William the day before) There's a good ball machine called a Lob-ster. It rained intermittently in the morning.
   We left for Beauchamp at 1.0.

__Saturday 9th September__
   Simon, Eve, Peter, John & Charles left for Charity's (Hatty's) christening at about 5.15.

On the way we passed through __Hartley Wintney__ + stopped to see Peter + Belinda. We shared supper on the kitchen table - we provided Bacon sandwiches + they contributed smoked salmon. We saw to goats, dogs, birds + rabbits too.
      On [the] to __Farnham__, + after the children were in bed on the drive we chatted to Mary + Richard, her parents, brother, [Lee/Bec], wife, Deana + their children while they all had lasagna. Richard Hendon was there too.
   The next [Sunday] day we walked to the church, It rained. George got lost twice. We went to the reception at [?Quenetter] + saw Jane + Harry, Sally + her family + David Maxine + the girls, Tim with a dreadful beard with his family + the new arrival Cains.
   We got home about 7pm.
~~Saturday~~ Friday October 22nd.
   The seven of us set off for __London__ at 10.20. We arrived at Pickett's lock, [?sa] [?Vallba leisure] centre. (Cinema) *** Depressing too, but nice to come home to a movie. The managers were abrupt.
   The weather was marvellous all 3 days.
      Simon, V+A ; Thomas Planetarium + Sims; William, Planetarium + Sims; Peter Planetarium +

British museum; John Planetarium + films.
Charles Planetarium + films, Eve V+A Ivories + Liberties.
   We had lunch + bought day tickets + went to the Victoria + Albert Museum. We saw beautiful ivories + an Elizabethan portrait. We had tea. We went into the Friends of the V+A room + had tea + saw [?Chad Hadkins].
   We tried to get tickets for Starlight Express at the Adelphi, but there weren't any. We saw the Chad lights in the bar upstairs.
   We bussed + trained back, queued for movie tickets + gobbled supper in the movies. E, T, W, P went to the Net; E, J, C, went to Pocahontas.
   Sunday We had a frustrating journey into London, + arrived in time for a sung [?Each] at Westminster Abbey, + glimpsed Peter Portal. We lunched at Pizza Hut + went on to the Planetarium. The films made Eve feel sick + brought on a migraine to add to her tooth ache.
   We had Tea watching tennis in Regents Park.
   We returned to the campsite.
TWSJC saw Apollo 13, E+P saw 9 Months. We had supper in the van before the movies

__Monday October 24th__
      We set off to Libertys & looked for material for Simon's dressing gown, bought Eve a rugby-watching brown coat & [illegible] velvet scarf. Peter bought a CA, John bought a candle.
      We had a delicious lunch at the Newman Arms, off Tottenham Court Rd.; Eve had steak & [?fried] kidney pudding & spotted dick & custard.
      We saw the Egyptian section & the peat bog man in the British Museum. Jane Total was out.
      We had an icecream in a park & trained & bussed back to the cinema car park.
      We are driving home up the motorway. ETA 7.50.

9th April 1996
Simon 46, Eve 42, Thomas 16, William 14
Peter 12, John 8, Charles 6. (7 next week).
      8.30. We set off for __Beauchamps__ for John to go on his first 'cello course. Peter is staying with Lawrence [?Barret] in France.
      Overcast, but slightly warmer.
      We are camping in the field.
      After lunch we drove to __Gloucester__ Cathedral. Gorgeous Norman [?arches] & fat pillars. Fan vaulting & old lead?.
      Font with [?] patterns on robes (12?) in lady chapel. Effigy of Robert Curthose elegant. Wood? painted red. William, Robert + William + Henry sons of William the Conqueror. Robert inherited Normandy. Robert William got England. Henry imprisoned Robert in Wales 1106–134, when Robert died & was buried at Gloucester.
      On to the Docks.
      We returned just before 4 as John finished his first day on the course.

[Glued-in printed leaflet: LADY CHAPEL

Built between 1457-1499 during the abbacies of Richard Hanley and William Farley, and standing on the site of a smaller building dating back to 1224. But originally the apsidal chapel of the Norman church, which was similar to those either side of the entrance, stood on this site. Chantry chapels with singing galleries form, as it were, small transepts. Christopher Whall designed and reglazed the windows (with one exception) 1898-1909.

This is the last of the major building works of the abbey period. It is a superb example of Perpendicular work (1470-1499) and judged by many to be the most beautiful Lady Chapel in the country. Everything about it speaks of shape and form, of ordered space, in which one can see grace and beauty, peace and harmony.

Either side is a wall of glass, with the stonework reduced to a tall slender shaft to provide support to the vault. The glass in the east window is from the 14th to early 16th centuries. In medieval times the windows were filled with figures, scores of them on either side, some half materialising, as it were, to become stone figures in niches up the wall shafts. The reredos too was filled with figures of the saints and brilliantly coloured.]

[Second partial printed leaflet fragment visible at right edge, partially obscured:
...W
...1089, the nave
...it survives in the
...nave replaced in
...style. West end
...1-1437.  Stained]

[Handwritten text in red/brown ink visible at far right margin, mostly obscured by gutter — partial fragments only:
...ve
...
...1...
...u...
...f...
...
...un

...
...Lo
...h...
...
...m
...D...
...p...
...R...
...ro
...ca
...T...
...
...pr...
...da...]

It rained in the early morning, so we set off to swim April 10". Charles was very brave jumping off the high board 18 ft. at Gloucester swimming pool. Unfortunately the chute was only open from 1-5 pm.
   We drove to a pub for lunch, where we'd been for a drink before - stone toad stools outside the door, where the river is liable to flood. The Red Lion. Lots of stuffed fish in glass cases. We had good sausage + chips + chocolate pud.
   On to Odda's Chapel, which Robert [?Grimley] had told Simon about, & St. Mary Deerhurst. Amazing old Saxon long & short quoins, splayed windows, carvings. Old Roman bricks recycled. Jacobean wood-carved pews in chancel. Brasses. An old font. The 2 best Saxon carving difficult to see, presumably, that's why they were not destroyed by Puritans.
   We came home via the ^Beauchamp Over fam[illegible — text cut off at page bottom]

shop, buying fruit + veg + looking at the goats + donkeys + 2 ostrichos.

	At Beauchamp, John was already sitting in the tent. We spent the evening playing ping pong + watching the boys playing achie 123. John hurt his knee, but it was better in the morning.

April 15
	A cold damp morning. We read + played ping pong. Simon + Eve walked up to the rail way line with Charles. By lunch time the sun had come out. John's concert was at 2.30. He played "Greensleeves" + the "Great Western" by Granny very well. Granny worked hard to keep John in the senior group for orchestra, because she did not realise that he was only 8.
	Home for supper.

__Sat Apl 20__
	We set off at 9.30. S, E, P, J, C, Daniela Fischer, the German, au pair, and Lawrence Basset, Peter's French exchange. Daniela + Lawrence are sharing a tent.
	We arrived at Tickhill near Doncaster at 11.30. to see border terriers, puppies. We can't decide whether to get a red or a blue. We'll return to pick one up from the next litter in August. Maybe we'll call him "Tonic."
	On through Doncaster to __Calver Mill__. It is a pleasant campsite, sheltered by hills in a dell with a raised canal alongside running to an old textile mill.
	After lunch we walked along the canal + river. A very good walk + it didn't rain. The loos are OK. Keyed. We have a new mat for outside the van - nylon. 10ft + 10 ft. to keep our feet dry. John + Charles ran laps around the site.
